statdown 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Why One HTML Engine Matters
  • Design

statdown: Use R in Your Static Site

Source: R/statdown-package.R
statdown.Rd

Renders .Rmd files to CommonMark .md that any static site generator can consume directly — no Pandoc, no second HTML engine. Your SSG stays in charge; R just provides the computed content.

Details

statdown is for bloggers who happen to use R, not R users who need a website. If you already have a Hugo, Jekyll, or Eleventy site, statdown lets you add R content without replacing your build pipeline.

Key Features

  • One engine — knitr evaluates R code and produces CommonMark. Your SSG handles everything else. No Pandoc in the middle means shortcodes, templates, and front matter all work as your SSG expects.

  • htmlwidgets work — interactive widgets (DT, leaflet, plotly) are rendered as inline HTML with dependencies managed by depkit.

  • CDN delivery — JS assets are served from jsDelivr with SRI integrity and automatic local fallback by default.

Usage

library(statdown)

# Render an .Rmd to .md for your SSG
statdown_render("posts/my-analysis.Rmd")

# Custom asset directory and URL prefix
statdown_render("posts/my-analysis.Rmd",
  output_root = "static/libs",
  url_root = "/libs")

On this page

Developed by Andrew Stryker.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
